Trilobite.geological_period: Cambrian to Permian (521-252 million years ago)
Trilobite.habitat: Marine ocean floors and shallow seas
Trilobite.notes: Trilobites are among the earliest known groups of arthropods, characterized by their three-lobed, three-segmented exoskeletons. They serve as important index fossils for dating Paleozoic rocks.

Ammonite.geological_period: Devonian to Cretaceous (400-66 million years ago)
Ammonite.notes: Ammonites are extinct marine mollusks with spiral shells divided into chambers. They are closely related to modern squids and octopuses and are often found in limestone deposits.

Belemnites.geological_period: Jurassic to Cretaceous (200-65 million years ago)
Belemnites.notes: Belemnites were squid-like cephalopods with internal bullet-shaped shells. Their fossils are often found as smooth, cylindrical rostra in sedimentary rocks.

Corals.geological_period: Ordovician to Present (485 million years ago – today)
Corals.notes: Corals are marine invertebrates that form large reef structures through calcium carbonate secretion. Fossil corals reveal ancient ocean conditions and climates.

Crinoids.geological_period: Ordovician to Present (480 million years ago - today)
Crinoids.notes: Crinoids, also called sea lilies, are echinoderms related to starfish. Their stalked fossils often resemble flower stems, found abundantly in Paleozoic rocks.

Leaf Fossil.geological_period: Jurassic to Recent (200 million years ago - today)
Leaf Fossil.estimated_lifespan_years: Varies by species (few years to decades)
Leaf Fossil.notes: Leaf fossils record the evolution of ancient plants and help scientists reconstruct past climates. Their impressions often show intricate vein patterns preserved in rock.
